For millennia, men sat in circles.

shoulder to shoulder,
heart to heart.

Today, most men sit in silence.

Let's Unwynd exists to change that.
Not once. Every month. For life.

Why this exists

Men today are quietly navigating one of the most profound transitions of their lives.

Between 35 and 53, everything shifts. Career, identity, relationships, energy. The city moves fast. The calendar stays full. And there is almost no real space to process any of it.

  • 01A friendship recession spreading quietly among urban Indian men.
  • 02Conversations that stay functional. Never meaningful.
  • 03A culture that rewards achievement but punishes presence.
  • 04Most men: surrounded, yet alone. Successful, yet searching.
One honest conversation changes more than one more scroll ↗
Question 01
When was the last time someone asked how you were. And you told the truth?
Not "fine." Not "busy." The actual truth. That's where we begin.
Question 02
Is the man you've become ready to meet who you're becoming?
You've done the first half. The doing. The proving. The achieving. Now navigate the second half together.
Question 03
What would change in your life if five men truly knew you?
Not colleagues. Not contacts. Men who've sat in the fire with you and held the space without flinching.
What Let's Unwynd is

An access programme. A method. A brotherhood in motion.

A peer-held space where every man comes as he is. He leaves with more clarity than he arrived with.

✦ This IS
  • ◦A structured quarterly access programme. Not a one-off event.
  • ◦A self-paced playbook to map your own direction.
  • ◦A dedicated 1-on-1 hour with someone who has been there.
  • ◦The Monthly Council: online and in-person Bengaluru.
  • ◦Deep, unmasked, honest conversation with men your age.
  • ◦Brotherhood built on presence, not performance.
✗ This is NOT
  • ×Therapy or clinical counselling.
  • ×Networking or business development.
  • ×A self-help program with scripts.
  • ×A room full of strangers performing wellness.
  • ×Advice. Fixing. Judgment. Small talk.
  • ×An afterthought. Something forgotten within the quarter.

Themes that emerge from real lived experience:

Health & VitalityRelationships & IntimacyCareer & PurposeFatherhoodSpiritualityAdventureIdentity & LegacyInspiring Stories
Who sits in this circle

Men from all walks of life. Bound by a shared life stage, not sameness.

You might be starting something new, rebuilding after a loss, questioning old paths, or quietly searching for what comes next.

01 ·
The Man Starting Over
New role, new city, new chapter. Rebuilding identity after a major shift.
02 ·
The Man at a Crossroads
Career at its peak. Yet asking: is this all there is?
03 ·
The Man Rebuilding
After loss, divorce, or failure. Choosing who to become next.
04 ·
The Restless Achiever
Successful on paper. Quietly restless. Looking for depth beyond function.
05 ·
The Searching Father
Navigating fatherhood and the question of what kind of man to model.
06 ·
The Quietly Courageous
No words yet. Something in you knows it's time to show up differently.
"Giving comes easy for men in midlife. Asking for what we need takes a lot of courage."
Quarterly Access

A Brotherhood That
Belongs to You

Purely Peer-Led. Entirely Not-for-Profit.

This isn't a product, a program, or a performance. It's a dedicated physical container for men navigating the complexities of midlife. We don't have facilitators or experts because the wisdom is in the room. We are simply a group of men in the thick of it, holding space for one another to show up, speak honestly, and figure it out together.

The Collective Entry

To maintain the integrity of the container, we do not offer public sign-ups. This is a curated circle of peers who are ready to show up for themselves and each other.

The Structure Peer-led sessions featuring three proprietary modules.
The Commitment 100% of your contribution goes toward the physical space rental.
The Philosophy No facilitators. No profit margins. Just a shared safe space.
Admission Only by invitation or referral.
Process Join the waitlist for our next quarterly intake.
Contribution ₹1499 / Quarter Unlocked only upon acceptance
Note. This is a non-profit brotherhood. Your contribution is strictly used to maintain our physical location, ensuring we have a consistent, real-world safe container to meet in.
The Three Proprietary Modules
Digital
Self-Paced
The Proprietary DIY Playbook
+ Nudge Cards

A structured workbook that maps where you are and where you want to go. Paired with Nudge Cards that prompt action between sessions. Works at your pace, in your privacy. Revisit as life shifts.

  • Works at your pace, in your privacy. Revisit as life shifts.
  • 52 Nudge Cards delivered. One prompt each week.
  • Covers identity, purpose, relationships, and work in one map.
Personal
One-to-One
60-Min Virtual
Alignment Call

A dedicated hour to walk through your Playbook, untangle sticking points, and sharpen what's next. Not therapy. A thinking session with a fellow brother who has navigated midlife.

  • Focused on your Playbook, not a generic catch-up.
  • Scheduled on-demand. A private space at your convenience.
  • You leave with two or three clear next moves, not a longer list.
Community
Monthly
The Monthly Council
+ Bengaluru IRL

Two formats, one rhythm. Virtual is a facilitated monthly reflective session. Bengaluru is in-person. Smaller. The kind of evening you will reference for months. F&B on actuals.

  • Virtual: open to all members, globally.
  • Bengaluru real-life: curated group, hosted venue.
  • Each session themed around what members are navigating.
Private WhatsApp Community
Members-only, active and moderated. The conversation continues between sessions.
Core Resource Library
Books, blogs, podcasts, and workshops curated for men in midlife.
Member-Only City Workshop
Forums and gatherings live in Bengaluru. Priority access for Quarterly Access members.

Coming Next Quarter

Modules launching soon.

Field trips, workshops, and expeditions. Available to all members from next quarter.

Local
Your Chapter, Your City
Field
Trips

Half-day or full-day outings organised through your local city chapter. Hikes, cookouts, sports, creative evenings. Whatever moves the men from the house into something worth recounting.

  • Organised locally so you don't have to travel far
  • Built-in crew. You never arrive alone.
Immersive
Hands-On, All Day
Work-
shops

Half-day or full-day deep dives into a skill, craft, or experience. Cook, build, brew, lead. Learn something that makes you more interesting, with men who take it seriously.

  • Hands-on. You leave with something tangible.
  • Small groups, everyone actually participates
Adventure
Stories You'll Tell in 10 Years
Exped-
itions

Multi-day trips to places worth going. Backcountry hikes, river runs, international travel. Experiences that push your limits. Stories you will still be telling a decade from now.

  • Curated for men who want to stretch themselves
  • The friendships built on these trips are a different level

Quarterly Access members get early access and priority booking when these modules go live.

What a session feels like

No agenda. No forced structure. A well-held container.

Members self-nominate themes from lived experience. The session stays fluid. Men connect best through shared activity and honest presence, not just talking.

  • ArrivalSlow landing into presence. Leave your week at the door.
  • Check-In Circle"Where am I right now?" One word or sentence from each man.
  • Shared ActivityWe move, make, or play together. Brotherhood is built through doing.
  • Deep ConversationsSmall-group dialogues around real, present-life tensions. No scripts, no fixing.
  • Open ReflectionShared insights, chosen silence, or stories. Whatever is honest.
  • ClosureYou carry something forward. A question. A commitment. A shift.

How we move past surface talk

The Mirror
One speaks; the other reflects only what they heard. No interpretation. No advice. Just feeling seen.
The Hot Seat
One man brings a real tension. The group asks probing questions only. No solutions. Surfaces truth fast.
The Compass
What do I think? Feel? What does my gut say? What action is emerging? Head. Heart. Gut. Aligned.
The Edge Round
"Something I'm avoiding / not saying / afraid to admit…" Brings radical honesty into the room.
The Ask
"I need" or "I'm asking for." Training men to articulate needs without armour.
What makes this space different

Presence is the price of entry.

The circle is kept small and curated so every voice matters. These principles are non-negotiable.

📵
Device-Free
No phones on the table. Presence is not optional. It is the entire point.
🔒
Invite-Only
You enter through an existing member or a brief conversation. The door is narrow by design.
🤝
Peer-to-Peer
No experts. No hierarchy. Every man earns his place through lived experience. Not credentials.
🌿
Fluid, Not Programmed
No rigid agenda. The container is held. What emerges inside it is real, not rehearsed.
🔢
Curated & Small
Small enough for every man to be known. Diverse enough for real perspectives.
🔐
What's Said Stays
Confidentiality is foundational. You can speak freely because nothing leaves the room.
🚫
No Advice. No Fixing.
Men are wired to solve. Here, we resist. You hold space. You do not fill it.
📅
Show Up. Don't Cancel.
Your absence creates a gap someone felt. If you choose to join, commit.
🏃
Activity & Movement
Men connect through doing, not just talking. Sessions are built for movement and shared experience.
The extended rhythm: Monthly in-person events. Weekly virtual check-ins. Quarterly shared experiences: treks, rides, runs, sport. A support network unlike anything most men have had before.
